system.description: Make life imitate art. Choose a genre of art or specific art piece you’ve interacted with before using Site Visit, yell “ACTION!” and roll [quality].
system.synopsis: Impose a work of art on reality

outcomes.success:
everyone and everything in the surrounding area adjusts to conform to the rules and conventions of that genre or work: A nearby police officer becomes a medieval knight; the casino is swarmed by large teams of professional thieves; a bustling park takes on the serene atmosphere of an impressionist painting.

This effect lasts for ninety minutes, until you dismiss it, or when somebody gets cut – whichever happens first. Whichever way this effect ends, when it is over all affected people assume that it was a particularly vivid flight of fancy.
outcomes.eachplus3: at any point while this effect is active, you may set a Scene by announcing something relevant to that genre. For example, you might say “this is where the villain gives his monologue” while approaching a target: they must stop whatever they’re doing to explain their history, motivation, and plan.
outcomes.triscendence: you may instead select any number of people and force them to participate in a line-for-line reenactment of a  script of your choice. This effect lasts until the end of the script is reached, or when you choose to end it.
outcomes.failure: the world around you adjusts to conform to the genre, but none of the people do. Everyone nearby is aware of what you’ve done; this may result in a huge amount of Loose Ends.
